decision_process: Approval or denial of a high-impact insurance claim using an AI recommendation
sampling_rule: All appealed and overridden decisions plus a fixed random sample of routine decisions

substantive_findings.rationale: Review logs show access to underlying case evidence before disposition in every sampled decision.
The sample contains independent reasons, alternatives, and documented disagreement with the AI recommendation.
Named reviewers could approve, reject, modify, stop, and escalate, and those powers were exercised.
The appeal process produced substantive reconsideration and reversals on the merits.
Substantiated harm led to corrected records, restitution, notification, and assigned remediation ownership.
The institution changed thresholds and workflow controls under named human authority after the incident.

telemetry_integrity.provenance: All artifacts have traceable source identifiers.
telemetry_integrity.completeness: All requested record classes were produced.
telemetry_integrity.edit_authority: Edits require a separately logged records-administration role.
telemetry_integrity.independence: Audit storage is operationally separate from the decision service.
telemetry_integrity.rationale: The record system provides sufficient provenance, separation, retention, and tamper evidence for the assessment.

summary: The fictional process shows substantive human influence across all six dimensions with adequate telemetry integrity.
limitations: Synthetic fixture; it does not establish institutional effectiveness.
